Teach In-Person Abroad

Best countries for classroom teaching with package deals

🇨🇳 China

Best package deal
$1,800–$3,500/mo
HousingFree apartment often included
FlightsReturn flight reimbursed
VisaZ visa (work)
RequiresBachelor's + TEFL

🇰🇷 South Korea

Most organized program
$1,600–$2,800/mo
HousingFree apartment
FlightsOne-way flight covered
VisaE-2 work visa
RequiresBachelor's + no TEFL

🇯🇵 Japan

JET Program — elite
$1,500–$2,500/mo
HousingSubsidized
FlightsNot always covered
VisaInstructor visa
RequiresBachelor's required

🇹🇭 Thailand

Lifestyle + adventure
$1,000–$2,000/mo
HousingNot included
FlightsNot covered
VisaNon-B visa
RequiresTEFL recommended

🇸🇦 Saudi Arabia

Maximum savings
$3,000–$6,000/mo
HousingFree housing
FlightsCovered both ways
VisaWork visa provided
RequiresBachelor's + 2yr exp

🇻🇳 Vietnam

Low cost, great life
$1,400–$2,400/mo
HousingNot included
FlightsSometimes covered
VisaWork permit
RequiresTEFL often enough

TEFL Quick Guide

From zero to earning abroad in under 3 months

01

Choose your TEFL

4–6 weeks

Go with a 120-hour accredited course. Best: TEFL.org, i-to-i, Bridge. Cost: $150–$400 online. Avoid free unaccredited certs.

02

Pick online or in-person

1 hour decision

Online pays $10–$40/hr from anywhere. In-person pays more but requires visa, flight, and relocation commitment.

03

Set up your profile

1 day

For online: record a 2-min intro video. Show your environment, energy, and approach. Profile photo matters immensely.

04

Get first students

2–4 weeks

Offer trial lessons at 50% off. Get 5 reviews, then raise rates. On iTalki/Preply this is 2–4 weeks.

05

Scale to full-time

3–6 months

20 students × $25/hr × 20hrs/wk = $2,000/mo. In Thailand this is a luxurious life. Scale to $3,500+ with premium rates.

Pair Teaching Income With the Right Country

$20/hr goes 3x further in some countries. Find yours with our AI quiz.
